_comment: One file per procedure_code. Distilled from the matching pair in PM JAY/Standard Treatment Guideline/master/<condition>/ : _Medco.pdf (clinical + mandatory docs) + _PPD.CPD.pdf (PPD/CPD processing checklists). Filename = procedure_code. This is what the Documentation Checker, Query Predictor, and Medical Justification agents read AFTER the picker has chosen the code.
min_doctor_qualification: MD equivalent (in General Medicine, Pediatric Medicine)
DNB equivalent (in General Medicine, Pediatric Medicine)
clinical_indications: Onset of symptoms is within 12 hours of exposure; usually following a household spraying.
Salivation, nausea, vomiting, diarrhoea, epigastric pain,
Sense of constriction around chest, dyspnoea,
Sweating, muscle twitching, fasciculations, flaccidity and muscle weakness
Convulsions, loss of reflexes and coma.

preauth.label: Clinical Notes including evaluation findings, indications for the procedure, and planned line of treatment
Red cell cholinesterase level, ECG, CBL, LFT, KFT reports (as applicable)
Copy of Medico legal certificate / FIR

claim.label: Detailed Indoor case papers with treatment given details

ppd_preauth.q: Was the clinical notes, signs, and symptoms, supporting with laboratory investigations are indicative of poisoning?
Were the Clinical Notes including evaluation findings, indications for the procedure, and planned line of treatment submitted?
Was the Red cell cholinesterase level, ECG, CBL, LFT, KFT (as applicable) submitted?
Was the Copy of Medico legal certificate / FIR submitted?

cpd_claim.q: Was the Detailed Indoor case papers with the Treatment details submitted?
Was the Detailed discharge summary submitted?

common_queries: Clinical notes, evaluation findings, indications for the procedure, or planned line of treatment not submitted or incomplete.
Red cell cholinesterase level, ECG, CBL, LFT, KFT reports (as applicable) not submitted or incomplete.
Copy of Medico legal certificate / FIR not submitted.
Clinical notes, signs, symptoms, or supporting laboratory investigations not indicative of poisoning.
Red cell cholinesterase level not reduced to 20% of normal values or not documented.

clinical_key_pointers: Proceed with Poisoning procedure only if diagnosis made is backed by clinical manifestation:
Stepwise care approach to a patient of poisoning is helpful in successful management.
